male_population: trained natural males with moderate body fat
carbohydrate_guidance: Allocate remaining calories to carbohydrate; place a meaningful portion around training when useful.
training_volume_adjustment: Retain productive volume; reduce 0-20% only if recovery declines.
training_intensity_adjustment: Retain moderate/heavy load exposure and target RIR.
cardio_guidance: Use the minimum cardio needed; increase gradually.
recovery_guidance: Prioritize sleep and manage rate of loss.
expected_outcome: Primarily fat loss with better lean-mass retention than aggressive restriction
adjustment_triggers: If loss exceeds target for 2 weeks or multi-lift performance declines, add calories or reduce fatigue.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Avoid aggressive restriction when already very lean, medically contraindicated, or showing low energy availability.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Carbohydrate based on preference and training performance after protein/fat targets.
training_volume_adjustment: Start at normal novice volume; avoid adding volume solely to burn calories.
training_intensity_adjustment: Progress where possible; stable strength is not the ceiling in novices.
cardio_guidance: Low-impact cardio may support the deficit.
recovery_guidance: Monitor joint tolerance, sleep, and adherence.
expected_outcome: Substantial fat loss with reasonable recomposition potential
adjustment_triggers: Reduce deficit if loss exceeds 1%/week repeatedly or recovery deteriorates.
recommended_duration: 8-24 weeks with maintenance breaks as needed
contraindications_or_exceptions: Medical obesity or comorbidities may require clinician-led nutrition care.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Preserve carbohydrate around demanding sessions when possible.
training_volume_adjustment: Retain high-value sets; reduce 10-30% when repeated recovery issues occur.
training_intensity_adjustment: Keep load exposure but avoid excessive failure work.
cardio_guidance: Conservative low-impact cardio; avoid abrupt increases.
recovery_guidance: Watch mood, libido, sleep, resting heart rate, and persistent performance loss.
expected_outcome: Slow fat loss with reduced but not eliminated lean-mass risk
adjustment_triggers: Stop or use maintenance if low-energy-availability symptoms or persistent regressions emerge.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Contest-level leanness carries health risks and is outside general fitness recommendations.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Adjust carbohydrate to activity and training quality.
training_volume_adjustment: Use productive or maintenance volume according to goal.
training_intensity_adjustment: Progress normally when recovery supports it.
cardio_guidance: Cardio for health and work capacity without compromising lifting.
recovery_guidance: Use stable weight to calibrate energy needs over at least 2 weeks.
expected_outcome: Weight stability with performance recovery or slow recomp
adjustment_triggers: Adjust calories 3-5% after at least 2 weeks outside desired weight band.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Short-term water shifts can mimic energy imbalance.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Support progressive training with sufficient carbohydrate.
training_volume_adjustment: Use a recoverable novice program, not high-volume specialization.
training_intensity_adjustment: Expect rep/load progression despite stable or falling scale weight.
recovery_guidance: Sleep 7-9 hours when feasible and maintain consistency.
expected_outcome: Fat loss with simultaneous lean-mass gain is plausible
adjustment_triggers: If waist and performance both stagnate 3-4 weeks, adjust calories or program after adherence audit.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Do not promise recomposition magnitude; measurement error is substantial.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Adequate carbohydrate to restore training performance.
training_volume_adjustment: Ramp from roughly 50-70% of prior volume over 2-4 weeks.
training_intensity_adjustment: Use conservative loads initially and progress technique first.
expected_outcome: Regain of prior muscle while maintaining or losing fat is plausible
adjustment_triggers: Slow the ramp if soreness affects next sessions or technique.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Prior injury or long layoff may require professional clearance.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Use carbohydrate to support high-quality training after protein/fat minimums.
training_volume_adjustment: Maintain productive volume; add sets only with evidence of need.
cardio_guidance: Keep cardio stable for health and appetite management.
recovery_guidance: Track long trends; avoid reacting to glycogen/water noise.
expected_outcome: Slow tissue gain with lower expected fat gain than aggressive surplus
adjustment_triggers: If gain exceeds 0.25%/week for 2-3 weeks with rapid waist gain, reduce calories 3-5%.
contraindications_or_exceptions: A surplus is not guaranteed to accelerate advanced hypertrophy.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

male_population: lean novice or intermediate natural males
carbohydrate_guidance: Carbohydrate should generally provide the remaining energy to support training.
training_volume_adjustment: Use status-appropriate productive volume; more food does not justify unrecoverable sets.
training_intensity_adjustment: Expect regular rep/load progress when technique is stable.
cardio_guidance: Keep cardio consistent rather than eliminating it.
recovery_guidance: Monitor weight and waist using 2-3 week trends.
expected_outcome: Muscle and strength gain with some fat gain
adjustment_triggers: Reduce surplus when rate exceeds status-specific target or waist rises without performance benefit.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Higher starting body fat may favor maintenance/recomposition before intentional surplus.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: No special carbohydrate allocation makes a large surplus muscle-specific.
training_intensity_adjustment: No evidence-based expectation of proportionally faster hypertrophy.
recovery_guidance: Monitor rapid waist and fat gain.
expected_outcome: Likely disproportionate fat gain for most trained natural males
adjustment_triggers: Reduce calories promptly when gain exceeds target without exceptional context.
contraindications_or_exceptions: May be appropriate only for clinically underweight males under professional guidance.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Distribute protein across 3-5 meals, often 0.3-0.55 g/kg per meal; total daily intake is primary.
recovery_guidance: Choose a pattern that supports adherence and sleep.
expected_outcome: Convenient coverage of daily protein with uncertain incremental benefit from perfect distribution
adjustment_triggers: Simplify meal timing if it harms adherence; do not exceed calories merely to hit meal targets.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Kidney disease or prescribed protein restriction requires clinician guidance.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

male_population: lean resistance-trained natural males in prolonged deficits
carbohydrate_guidance: Raise carbohydrate primarily while keeping protein stable; preserve the planned average deficit unless taking a maintenance break.
training_volume_adjustment: Maintain or slightly reduce volume according to fatigue.
training_intensity_adjustment: Use restored carbohydrate to support performance, not forced PRs.
cardio_guidance: Keep cardio stable or reduce briefly if fatigue-driven.
recovery_guidance: Use for adherence, performance, or fatigue management rather than claimed metabolic reset.
expected_outcome: Possible short-term performance/adherence benefit; body-composition superiority is uncertain
adjustment_triggers: Return to continuous dieting if breaks trigger loss of control or erase the intended deficit.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Evidence is small and mixed-sex; not required for successful fat loss.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

carbohydrate_guidance: Increase calories/carbohydrate if loss is above target and performance repeatedly declines.
training_volume_adjustment: First remove low-value fatigue; do not remove all heavy exposure.
training_intensity_adjustment: Treat stable strength as success; use multi-session trends.
cardio_guidance: Reduce added cardio if it coincides with recovery decline.
expected_outcome: Preserve muscle by correcting overly aggressive energy or fatigue load
adjustment_triggers: If two or more lifts regress for 2+ comparable sessions and weight loss is above target, add 5-10% calories or reduce expenditure.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Pain or illness requires separate evaluation.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

training_volume_adjustment: Do not apply natural-male dose or gain expectations as validated for enhanced males.
expected_outcome: Population is explicitly separated; no combined recommendation
adjustment_triggers: Do not infer from natural-male tables.
contraindications_or_exceptions: Non-medical anabolic drug use has material health risks and is outside this database's prescriptive scope.
notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.
